title: Slice-tower convergence justified with the wrong connectivity invariant

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
In the paragraph 'That the tower converges (for $E$ connective ...)', convergence is justified by claiming 'the Postnikov connectivity of $G_+\otimes_H N^TS^1$ is $|T/H|\to\infty$ with $|T|$'.

Two problems:
(1) $|T/H|$ is the GEOMETRIC connectivity at the top, NOT the ordinary (Postnikov) connectivity. The underlying spectrum of $N^TS^1\cong S^{\mathbb R\cdot T}$ is $S^{|T|}$, so the relevant ordinary connectivity grows like $|T|$ (up to a factor from induction), not $|T/H|$.
(2) Convergence $E\xrightarrow{\sim}\varprojlim P^n_{\mathcal O}E$ for connective $E$ requires showing $\mathrm{conn}(P_{\mathcal O,n}E)\to\infty$ in the ordinary t-structure; this is asserted with an incorrect supporting invariant and without any estimate.

Suggested fix: prove $\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n}\subseteq\mathrm{Sp}^G_{\ge\lceil n/|G|\rceil}$ (the underlying sphere of $G_+\otimes_H N^TS^1$ is built from $S^{|T|}$ with $|T|\ge n$, and induction lowers connectivity boundedly), then run the standard slice-tower convergence argument (HHR Â§4 / Hill primer).

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
## Re-review: addressed in current `solution.tex`.
`thm:converge` now derives a uniform geometric bound $\mathrm{gconn}(F_n)(K)\ge (n+1)/r_K\ge (n+1)/|G|$ for ALL $K$ from `lem:forward` (applied to $F_n=P^{\mathcal O}_{n+1}E\in\tau_{\ge n+1}$, which is justified by `lem:cover-conn`), upgrades to genuine connectivity via `lem:detect-conn` (eq:conn-ge), and concludes via a Milnor $\lim^1$ sequence with eventually-zero towers. The correct invariant (the slice-degree/index-scaled gconn over all subgroups) is used. This gap is closed.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
## Audit confirms the gap is closed; no edits needed.

I re-examined the convergence argument and its supporting lemmas against both objections.

**Objection (1) â€” wrong invariant ($|T/H|$ used as ordinary connectivity).** The convergence proof (`thm:converge`) no longer uses $|T/H|$ as an ordinary-connectivity bound. A text search confirms the offending phrase ("Postnikov connectivity of $G_+\otimes_H N^TS^1$ is $|T/H|$") is gone. The four remaining occurrences of $|T/H|$ are all legitimate: three are the *geometric* computation $\Phi^H(N^{H/\chi(H)}S^1)\simeq S^{|T/H|}$ (correct, via $\Phi^H S^V\simeq S^{V^H}$), and one is the structural inequality $|T|\le r_H\cdot|T/H|$ relating cardinality to orbit count.

**Objection (2) â€” convergence needs $\mathrm{conn}(P_{\mathcal O,n}E)\to\infty$ with a genuine estimate.** This is now supplied rigorously through the correct (index-scaled) invariant:
- `lem:cover-conn`: $F_n=P^{\mathcal O}_{n+1}E\in\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge n+1}$ (uses connectivity of $E$).
- `lem:forward`: every $X\in\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge m}$ satisfies $r_K\cdot\mathrm{gconn}(X)(K)\ge m$ for **all** $K\subseteq G$, where $r_K=[K:\chi^{\mathcal O}(K)]\le|K|\le|G|$. Hence $\mathrm{gconn}(F_n)(K)\ge (n+1)/r_K\ge (n+1)/|G|$.
- `lem:detect-conn` (eq:conn-ge): $\mathrm{conn}(X^H)\ge\min_{K\subseteq H}\mathrm{gconn}(X)(K)$, upgrading the geometric bound to a genuine one: $\mathrm{conn}(F_n^H)\ge (n+1)/|G|\to\infty$.
- A Milnor $\varprojlim^1$ sequence with eventually-zero towers then gives $\varprojlim_n F_n\simeq 0$, hence $E\xrightarrow{\sim}\varprojlim_n P^n_{\mathcal O}E$.

The crucial conversion the critic asked for â€” that $\tau^{\mathcal O}_{\ge m}$ lands in genuinely $\lceil m/|G|\rceil$-connective spectra â€” is exactly what `lem:forward` + `lem:detect-conn` provide (with explicit constant $|G|$, since $r_K\le|G|$). The correct invariant (index-scaled geometric connectivity over all subgroups), not the bare top geometric connectivity $|T/G|$, drives convergence.

I verified internally: $r_K=[K:\chi(K)]\le|K|$ since $\chi(K)\subseteq K$ is a nontrivial subgroup; all referenced labels (`lem:forward`, `lem:cover-conn`, `lem:detect-conn`/eq:conn-ge) exist and state what is invoked. (LaTeX compile skipped: no toolchain in sandbox.)

Resolving.
